sections.understanding:
Akiba Commercial Bank PLC (ACB), founded in August 1997 by over 300 Tanzanian entrepreneurs as a micro-finance initiative and now operating 14 branch locations across Tanzania, has issued Supplier Prequalification Notice ACB/SPQ/2026-2027. This is a two-year prequalification exercise covering 40 distinct supply and service categories, ranging from ICT equipment and enterprise software to civil works, professional consultancy, and marketing. Prequalification is conducted exclusively online via the TenderSure™ platform at https://www.tendersure.africa/, with a non-refundable registration fee of TSH 100,000 per category applied for. Successful prequalification does not guarantee business but establishes the supplier on ACB's approved vendor list for procurement consideration throughout the 2026–2027 period.

Of the 40 categories, ZIMA Solutions Limited is directly and technically qualified to apply for multiple ICT-aligned categories: Category 4 (Supply, Servicing and Support of CCTV Camera Systems), Category 5 (Supply of ICT Equipment — Desktops, Laptops, Printers and Allied Accessories), Category 6 (Supply of ICT Software and Enterprise Solutions), Category 23 (Servicing and Maintenance of HP Servers), Category 26 (Provision of Network Infrastructure Services and Structured Cabling), Category 27 (Provision of Website Development and Hosting Services), and Category 36 (Provision of Vehicle Tracking Systems). Each of these maps precisely to ZIMA's core service offering and existing delivery track record.

The prequalification exercise is open to both new and existing suppliers, with the important note that existing ACB suppliers must also re-participate to remain on the approved vendor list. This open, competitive structure means ZIMA must demonstrate technical competence, organizational credibility, and financial standing — all of which are supported by formal BRELA registration, a valid business license from Kinondoni Municipal Council, a registered TIN, and a documented portfolio of 20+ enterprise projects across 50+ client organizations.

Akiba Commercial Bank's core focus on micro-finance and financial inclusion for Tanzanians aligns naturally with ZIMA's own mission of digital transformation for financial institutions. ACB's 14-branch network requires robust, scalable ICT infrastructure — including server management, network cabling, CCTV systems, enterprise software, and digital platforms — all areas where ZIMA has active, measurable delivery experience including 4 live Bank of Tanzania (BOT) system integrations running at 99.9% uptime.

The prequalification process via TenderSure™ is transparent and standardized, requiring suppliers to demonstrate competency per category. ZIMA's approach is to apply for all seven ICT-aligned categories, presenting full legal documentation (Certificate of Incorporation No. 181314605, Business License No. BL01396912024-2500021919, TIN 181-314-605) and a structured capability statement for each category to maximize qualification probability across ACB's entire ICT procurement scope.
sections.why_us:
ZIMA Solutions Limited is incorporated under Tanzania's Companies Act 2002, with Certificate of Incorporation No. 181314605 issued by BRELA on 17th January 2025. The company holds a valid Business License (No. BL01396912024-2500021919) issued by Kinondoni Municipal Council for ICT Services Local, with the current license period running from 20th January 2025 to 19th January 2026. The company is fully tax-compliant under TIN 181-314-605. These credentials satisfy ACB's requirement for competent, formally registered suppliers.

ZIMA's relevance to ACB's ICT procurement needs is direct and demonstrable. We have delivered 20+ enterprise projects for 50+ organizations, including live integrations with the Bank of Tanzania's RTGS (Real-Time Gross Settlement), TIPS (Tanzania Instant Payment System), GePG (Government e-Payment Gateway), and NIDA (National ID verification) platforms — all operating at 99.9% uptime. This track record in mission-critical, high-availability financial system environments is directly applicable to serving ACB's 14-branch network, where system reliability and uptime directly impact customer service delivery and regulatory compliance.

For Category 6 (ICT Software and Enterprise Solutions) specifically, ZIMA has built and deployed Loan Management Systems, SACCO Management Systems, Microfinance Systems, HR Management Systems, and API Gateways — all directly relevant to a commercial bank like ACB. For Categories 4 and 26 (CCTV and Network Infrastructure), ZIMA has hands-on infrastructure deployment experience across financial and enterprise environments. For Category 27 (Website Development and Hosting), ZIMA develops and hosts secure web platforms for regulated financial institutions, understanding the compliance and uptime requirements that banking web presence demands.

ZIMA is 100% Tanzanian-owned, headquartered in Dar es Salaam (Makongo, Near Ardhi University, Kinondoni), with directors Andrew Stanslaus Mashamba and Caroline Ceasar Shija. As a local ICT firm focused exclusively on Tanzania and East Africa, ZIMA brings the on-the-ground responsiveness, local regulatory knowledge, and long-term commitment to partnership that international or Nairobi-based vendors cannot match for an institution like ACB serving Tanzanian communities.
approach: Phase 1: TenderSure Registration and Category Selection (Week 1) — Create supplier account on TenderSure™ platform at https://www.tendersure.africa/. Identify and confirm the seven target categories: 4, 5, 6, 23, 26, 27, and 36. Pay TSH 100,000 per category (total TSH 700,000) via available mobile money or card payment channels. Deliverable: Confirmed registration with payment receipts for all seven categories.

Phase 2: Documentation Preparation per Category (Week 1–2) — Compile category-specific capability statements, referencing Certificate of Incorporation No. 181314605, Business License No. BL01396912024-2500021919, TIN 181-314-605, and the Memorandum and Articles of Association. Prepare project reference lists, technical specifications, and staffing profiles tailored to each category's scope. Deliverable: Complete, category-specific prequalification dossier for each of the seven categories.

Phase 3: Online Submission via TenderSure™ (Week 2) — Upload all required documents per category through the TenderSure™ portal prior to the deadline. Ensure each submission is complete, accurate, and cross-referenced across categories where documentation overlaps (e.g., corporate registration applies to all). Deliverable: Confirmed TenderSure™ submission receipts for all seven categories.

Phase 4: Follow-Up and Query Response (Post-Submission) — Monitor registered email for evaluation outcome notifications from TenderSure™ per their stated process. Respond promptly to any clarification requests from the TenderSure™ evaluation team or ACB Procurement Department (info@acbbank.co.tz / 0800 750 336). Deliverable: Timely responses to any queries, maintaining active candidacy across all categories.

Phase 5: Post-Prequalification Engagement (Upon Approval) — Upon successful prequalification in target categories, proactively engage ACB's Procurement Department to introduce ZIMA's specific product portfolio — particularly enterprise banking software, HP server maintenance capabilities, CCTV deployment, and network infrastructure services — positioning ZIMA as ACB's preferred ICT partner for the 2026–2027 procurement cycle. Deliverable: Initial meeting with ACB procurement stakeholders and a tailored ICT capability presentation.
sections.value_proposition:
ZIMA Solutions Limited offers ACB a single, accountable Tanzanian ICT partner across seven of its prequalification categories, eliminating the procurement overhead of managing multiple vendors for interconnected ICT needs. A CCTV system, server infrastructure, network cabling, and enterprise software are not independent assets — they are interdependent components of a bank's operational technology stack. ZIMA's cross-category competence means ACB benefits from integrated deployments, consistent service quality standards, and a vendor who understands the full picture rather than optimizing a single component in isolation.

For a bank whose founding mission is serving Tanzanian entrepreneurs and micro-finance clients, ZIMA's local presence and regulatory fluency are material advantages. ZIMA has active, operational BOT system integrations — the same regulatory environment ACB operates within. This means ZIMA understands BOT compliance requirements, data handling obligations under Tanzania's data protection framework, and the uptime expectations of a regulated financial institution. No international vendor can offer this depth of local regulatory alignment without significant onboarding investment.

ZIMA's pricing model is structured for sustainable, long-term partnership rather than one-off engagements. For a two-year prequalification period like ACB/SPQ/2026-2027, ZIMA can offer retainer-based service agreements for server maintenance (Category 23) and network infrastructure (Category 26), ensuring predictable costs for ACB while guaranteeing dedicated support capacity. Combined with ZIMA's 99.9% uptime track record on live financial systems, this represents a materially lower total cost of ownership than reactive, incident-based procurement from multiple smaller vendors.
compliance_notes: Category 4 — Supply, Servicing and Support of CCTV Camera Systems: ZIMA has ICT infrastructure deployment experience including CCTV systems for enterprise and financial institution environments. We supply, install, configure, and provide ongoing maintenance for IP-based CCTV systems across multi-branch deployments comparable to ACB's 14-branch network.

Category 5 — Supply of ICT Equipment (Desktops, Laptops, Printers and Allied Accessories): ZIMA sources and supplies branded ICT hardware through established vendor relationships. All equipment supplied meets manufacturer specifications and is accompanied by valid warranty documentation.

Category 6 — Supply of ICT Software and Enterprise Solutions: ZIMA is a software development and deployment firm with a portfolio of Loan Management Systems, SACCO Management Systems, API Gateways, HR Systems, and Microfinance platforms — all enterprise-grade solutions directly applicable to a commercial bank's operational requirements.

Category 23 — Servicing and Maintenance of HP Servers: ZIMA's technical team has hands-on experience with HP server infrastructure in financial institution environments, including preventive maintenance, firmware updates, performance monitoring, and incident response for mission-critical banking systems.

Category 26 — Provision of Network Infrastructure Services and Structured Cabling: ZIMA delivers end-to-end network infrastructure including structured cabling, switch configuration, firewall deployment, and LAN/WAN setup for enterprise clients. Our work on multi-branch financial institution networks is directly applicable to ACB's branch connectivity needs.

Category 27 — Provision of Website Development and Hosting Services: ZIMA designs, develops, and hosts secure web platforms for regulated financial institutions, with full understanding of SSL/TLS requirements, uptime SLAs, and the compliance standards expected of a banking web presence in Tanzania.

Category 36 — Provision of Vehicle Tracking Systems: ZIMA develops and integrates IoT-based tracking and monitoring solutions. Vehicle tracking systems are within our IoT and systems integration capability, deployable for ACB's fleet management needs.

Legal Registration: ZIMA Solutions Limited is duly incorporated under the Companies Act 2002, Certificate No. 181314605, issued by BRELA on 17th January 2025. This satisfies ACB's requirement for formally registered, competent suppliers.

Business License: Business License No. BL01396912024-2500021919, issued by Kinondoni Municipal Council for ICT Services Local, valid 20th January 2025 to 19th January 2026, confirms ZIMA's legal authorization to operate ICT services in Tanzania.
Tax Compliance: TIN 181-314-605 confirms ZIMA's registration with the Tanzania Revenue Authority. Full tax compliance documentation is available for submission via the TenderSure™ platform.

Online Process Compliance: ZIMA will conduct the entire prequalification through the TenderSure™ platform as mandated, paying the TSH 100,000 non-refundable fee per category and submitting all documentation digitally per the platform's requirements.
sections.technical_approach:
For ICT infrastructure categories (CCTV, network cabling, server maintenance), ZIMA follows a structured site survey → design → deployment → commissioning → handover methodology. For ACB's 14-branch network, this would involve an initial infrastructure audit to map existing cabling topology, server specifications, and CCTV coverage gaps, followed by a phased deployment plan that minimizes operational disruption during banking hours. All network and server work is performed with change management documentation and rollback plans, a standard ZIMA practice inherited from our BOT integration work where zero-downtime deployments are contractually required.

For software and enterprise solution categories (Category 6), ZIMA employs an agile delivery methodology with sprint-based milestones, UAT checkpoints, and formal sign-off processes. Our enterprise solutions are built on scalable architectures — typically Python/FastAPI backends, React frontends, and PostgreSQL databases — with API-first design that enables integration with ACB's existing core banking system. All software deliverables include technical documentation, user training, and a 90-day post-go-live hypercare period as standard.

For vehicle tracking (Category 36), ZIMA integrates GPS hardware with a centralized web dashboard providing real-time fleet visibility, geofencing alerts, route history, and utilization reporting. The platform is hosted on ZIMA's managed cloud infrastructure with 99.9% uptime SLA, and access is provided via a secure web portal requiring no client-side software installation — suitable for deployment across ACB's administrative offices.
sections.risk_management:
The primary risk for ZIMA in this prequalification is the recency of incorporation (January 2025), which means the company has less than two years of formal operating history at the time of application. ZIMA mitigates this by presenting a substantive project portfolio — 20+ enterprise deliveries across 50+ organizations, including 4 live BOT integrations — that demonstrates operational maturity disproportionate to corporate age. The founding team's prior experience in financial technology delivery predates the formal incorporation, and reference clients are available to validate delivery quality directly to ACB's evaluation team. Additionally, ZIMA's Business License (BL01396912024-2500021919) and TIN registration confirm active, compliant operations since January 2025.

A secondary risk is category breadth — applying across seven categories could raise questions about resource capacity. ZIMA addresses this by maintaining a clear service tiering: Categories 6 and 27 (software and web development) are ZIMA's primary competency with dedicated in-house engineering teams; Categories 4, 5, 23, and 26 (hardware supply, CCTV, servers, and networking) are supported by established vendor and subcontractor partnerships with documented track records. Category 36 (vehicle tracking) leverages ZIMA's existing IoT integration framework. For any category where a specialized subcontractor is engaged, ZIMA retains primary contract accountability, SLA ownership, and client-facing responsibility — ACB engages a single accountable party regardless of the delivery model.
